tips.summary: Insert at line ends I / A
details: A appends at end of line

tips.summary: Insert at true line start gI
details: Unlike I, it does not skip indentation
o opens below and enters Insert
O opens above and enters Insert
gi jumps to where you last left Insert

tips.summary: One-shot Normal command from Insert Ctrl-o
details: In Insert: Ctrl-o then one Normal command

tips.summary: Start IdeaVim Tutor from the status-bar Vim icon
details: Click the bottom-right Vim indicator in the IDE status bar

tips.summary: Map CapsLock as a dual-role Esc/Ctrl key
details: Tap for Esc, hold for Ctrl
Cuts hand movement for Vim commands
Use Karabiner, keyd, Kanata or similar

tips.summary: Delete previous word while typing Insert Ctrl-w
details: Deletes word left of cursor in Insert mode
Faster than Esc + dw for small fixes

tips.summary: Clear back while typing Insert Ctrl-u
details: Deletes back to start of the insert line
Great for rewriting the current line
Ctrl-r redoes the last undone change
Dot repeats the last text-changing action
Do one clean change, then move and press .

tips.summary: Home-row movement h j k l
details: h left, j down, k up, l right
Put a number before a command
G goes to the last line
e moves to end of word
ge goes to end of previous word

tips.summary: Big WORD moves W / B / E
details: Great for paths and long tokens
gE goes to end of previous WORD
g_ jumps to last non-space character
These are motions; use with operators (e.g., d+)
H top of screen, M middle, L bottom

tips.summary: Recenter cursor line zt / zz / zb
details: zt top, zz center, zb bottom
Use after searches/jumps to keep context

tips.summary: Scroll window without moving cursor Ctrl-e / Ctrl-y

details: Controls how many lines Ctrl-d/Ctrl-u scroll

tips.summary: Move by screen lines on wraps gj / gk
details: gj moves down one displayed line

tips.summary: Screen-line start/end on wraps g0 / g^ / g$

details: { and } stop treating every blank line as a hard break
Useful in code with indented blank separators
qa starts recording into register a
Press q again to stop recording

tips.summary: Append to an existing macro qA
details: qA keeps recording at the end of macro a
Useful when a macro is close but needs one more step
Use to inspect a whole block quickly

tips.summary: Extend % to tags and control blocks
details: Use % on if/else/end or HTML/XML tags
'' returns to previous line (column may change)
Ctrl-o goes back, Ctrl-i goes forward
Works after searches, gd, and big jumps
'. jumps to the line of the last change

tips.summary: Linewise bounds of last changed/yanked text '[ and ']
details: '[ jumps to the start line of last changed/yanked text
'] jumps to the end line of last changed/yanked text

tips.summary: Exact bounds of last changed/yanked text `[ and `]
details: `[ jumps to exact start of last changed/yanked text
`] jumps to exact end of last changed/yanked text
Press Enter to search, Esc to cancel
n next match, N previous match
Like * and # but allows partial matches

tips.summary: Case toggle inside a pattern \c and \C
Keep results centered while hopping nzz
details: Use Nzz to go backward and recenter

tips.summary: Use search as a motion with operators
details: d/pat<Enter> deletes up to next match
c?pat<Enter> changes back to previous match
Good for paths, JSON, and punctuation

tips.summary: Use \v to reduce escaping in regex
Repeat last substitute on a line &
details: & repeats the last :s on the current line

tips.summary: Use \r to insert a newline in :s
details: Handy for reformatting lists and code

tips.summary: Use & to reuse the whole match in :s
details: Replacement '&' means the entire matched text
Capture groups in pattern, then reuse in replacement
{count}@{reg} repeats a macro in a register
Example 10@a runs macro a ten times
Use :normal @r to apply on each line of a range

tips.summary: Fast rename loop * then cw then n .
details: cw to change, then n . to repeat

tips.summary: Replace matches one by one with cgn
details: cgn changes the next match and enters Insert
Press . to apply the same edit to later matches

tips.summary: Find a character forward on the line f{c}
details: f; jumps to next ';' on the line

tips.summary: Find a character backward on the line F{c}
details: F; jumps to previous ';' on the line
Use to move precisely without searching

tips.summary: Move till before a character t{c}
Move till after a character backward T{c}
details: Good for edits near opening brackets

tips.summary: Jump by two chars with sneak
details: sfo jumps to the next fo; Sfo searches backward
Great for fast local jumps without /

tips.summary: Delete/change to line start d0 / c0
details: d0 deletes to absolute line start
c0 changes to absolute line start

tips.summary: Delete/change to first non-blank d^ / c^
Delete/change to end of line d$ / c$
details: d$ deletes to end of line
c$ changes to end of line

tips.summary: Shortcut to end-of-line ops D / C
Operator + motion is the core pattern
details: d{motion} deletes, c{motion} changes, y{motion} yanks
s replaces one char and enters Insert
S replaces the whole line and enters Insert
S is the same as cc
r. replaces char under cursor with '.'
cw changes from cursor to end of word
dw deletes from cursor to start of next word
J joins and inserts one space
gJ joins without inserting a space

tips.summary: Change case over a motion gu / gU
Toggle case over a motion g~
details: g~iw toggles case of current word
Ctrl-x decrements it (IDE may need key conflict fix)

tips.summary: Renumber repeated values with g Ctrl-a
details: Use on a Visual selection of numbers
First match gets +1, next gets +2, then +3
Use g Ctrl-x to step the selection downward
Prefer operator+motion edits like cw or ciw
Do one clean change, then move

tips.summary: Delete to next word start vs end dw / de
details: dw deletes to start of next word
de deletes to end of current/next word

tips.summary: Delete a word plus space daw

details: Works even if cursor is mid-word

tips.summary: Change a word plus space caw

details: yaw copies word plus trailing space
yy, yiw, and other yanks briefly highlight the copy
Helpful when you want visual confirmation
yw yank word, y$ yank to end of line
y0 yanks to start of line
y$ yanks to end of line
p puts after cursor (or below for linewise)
P puts before cursor (or above for linewise)

tips.summary: Paste and leave cursor after gp / gP
details: gp pastes and moves to end of pasted text
gP does the same for paste-before
Paste it with "0p even after deletes
"1 usually holds the most recent delete
Paste with "1p after an accidental dd
"ayiw yanks a word into register a
Keeps your unnamed register free for newer yanks
gr{motion} replaces text with the current register
"agrw uses register a for the replacement

tips.summary: Append to a named register with "A
details: "A appends into register a instead of replacing it
Use after "ayy or "ayiw to collect snippets
Paste the combined text later with "ap
"- stores short deletes smaller than one line
Useful after x, dw, or other small cuts

tips.summary: Use clipboard+=unnamedplus for default clipboard ops
details: Use the system clipboard by default for yank/paste
Routes clipboard p/P through IntelliJ insertion
Useful when IDE paste handlers should stay in play

tips.summary: Insert register while typing Insert Ctrl-r
Insert register in command line Ctrl-r
details: In : or /, Ctrl-r inserts register content
Useful when you forgot what you yanked
:put a inserts register a below the cursor
:put! a inserts it above the cursor
Also works in Insert with Ctrl-r =
zf{motion} folds a motion, like zfip
Requires IDE code folding to be enabled
zo opens a fold, zc closes a fold
za toggles a fold (IdeaVim: works where IDE folding supports it)
zj moves to the start of the next fold
zk moves to the start of the previous fold
x deletes char under cursor, p puts it after
`==` fixes indentation on the current line
`=ip` fixes indentation for the current paragraph

tips.summary: Enter Visual mode v / V / Ctrl-v

details: o moves cursor to other end of selection
In blockwise Visual: O swaps horizontal corner
gv reselects the last Visual selection
`[ jumps to the start of the last change
v enters Visual and `] reaches the end
Useful after paste, indent, or autoformat
Then press . to repeat on next selection/line
Select a column, press I, type text
Esc applies the insert to all selected lines
Select a column, press A, type text
Esc applies the append to all selected lines
Select a block, press c, type replacement
Esc applies to all selected lines

tips.summary: Block replace with one char Ctrl-v then r
details: Select block, press r then a char
Replaces all selected chars with that char

tips.summary: Use Visual range for Ex commands
Quick select inner word/paragraph viw / vip

details: gg to top, V linewise select, G to bottom
Then run =, :sort, or :s on the range

tips.summary: Join lines with IDE-aware behavior ideajoin
details: Use :set ideajoin for IDE smart join
Then J uses IDE smart join rules
Without ideajoin, J uses Vim join rules

tips.summary: Comment code with gcc and gc motions
details: gcc comments a line; gcap comments a paragraph
gc in Visual comments the selection

tips.summary: Add or change pairs with surround
details: ysiw) adds (); cs"' swaps quote types
cxiw marks text; repeat on a second target to swap
Use cxx to exchange whole lines
Works even when y/p is awkward
Use ranges + destination for quick duplication

tips.summary: Run Normal commands on a range :normal
details: Example: :%normal A; appends ; to every line
Test on a small range first

tips.summary: Global substitute only on matching lines
details: Acts only on lines matching ERROR
In : prompt: Ctrl-w deletes a word
Ctrl-u clears the whole command line
In IdeaVim, this maps to IDE save
In IDE, typically saves all open editors
Quick way to flip UI settings
Use mode-specific maps to debug conflicts
Use Ctrl-] to jump tags, Ctrl-t to jump back

tips.summary: Text objects: inside vs around i / a
details: i{obj} excludes delimiters, a{obj} includes them
Cursor can be anywhere inside string
Handy in Markdown and JS templates
Use i for inside and a for around
Combine with d, c, y, or v like ciw
cib is an alias for the same object

tips.summary: Edit inside braces ci{ and ciB
details: ciB is an alias for braces in many setups

tips.summary: Delete inside vs around pairs di( and da(
details: Same pattern works for {}, [], and quotes

tips.summary: Select inside/around pairs vi( and va{

details: cia changes one argument; daa deletes one
Great for calls with nested parentheses

tips.summary: Select the whole file with ae / ie
details: vae or vie selects the whole buffer

tips.summary: Target an indent block with ii / ai
details: vai selects the current indent block; dii deletes it
Useful in Python and nested code
Works with c, d, and y like cis or das

tips.summary: Fold all / unfold all zM / zR
details: zM closes all folds in view
zR opens all folds (IdeaVim: depends on IDE folding support)
cit changes inside an HTML/XML tag
dat deletes the tag and its content
Requires tag text-object support in your setup
vit selects text inside a tag
vat selects the tag and its text
:split opens the current buffer in a horizontal split
Use when you want to compare or edit in stacked views
:vsplit opens the current buffer in a vertical split

tips.summary: Split from the keyboard Ctrl-w s / Ctrl-w v
details: Ctrl-w s makes a horizontal split
Ctrl-w v makes a vertical split
Useful when you want split creation without typing : commands

tips.summary: Open the project tree with NERDTree

details: ma sets a mark, then go explore
ma sets mark a in this file
Use mA for a global mark; ideamarks can sync A-Z to IDE bookmarks

tips.summary: Sync global marks with IDE bookmarks
details: Capital-letter marks sync with IDE bookmarks
Overwrite a mark to update it
Ctrl-^ is Ctrl-6 on many keyboards
Use :buffer {N} to jump to a buffer by number
:bn goes to next buffer (often next IDE tab)
Use when you want to keep splits but close a file
Use when you want to keep other splits

tips.summary: Close all other splits Ctrl-w o
details: Makes the current split the only one
:only closes every other split in the current tab
It is the Ex-command form of Ctrl-w o
Ctrl-w w moves to the next split
Useful when you just need the next pane, not a specific direction

tips.summary: Move to a split Ctrl-w h/j/k/l
details: Best when you already know which neighboring split you want
Ctrl-w = makes split sizes even again
Helpful after a lot of manual resizing

tips.summary: Maximize a split Ctrl-w _ and Ctrl-w |

details: Remap if your IDE keymap intercepts them
gd goes to definition (IDE action if enabled)
gD goes to declaration (if IDE provides it)
:tabnew opens a fresh Vim tab page
Useful when you want a separate split layout
Use it when you want to keep the rest of your tab layout

tips.summary: Next and previous tab gt / gT
details: gt goes to the next tab
gT goes to the previous tab

tips.summary: Jump to a tab by number {count}gt
details: 2gt jumps to tab 2, 5gt jumps to tab 5
Useful when you have several tab pages open
Use it when you need an overview before switching

tips.summary: Next and previous tab with Ex :tabn / :tabp

details: Use it to reduce a crowded tab layout quickly
:tabmove moves to end when you omit the number
:actionlist and track action IDs help find IDs
Useful when a plugin conflicts with Vim keys

tips.summary: Use gi with g; / g, for edit loops
details: gi returns to the last insert position
g; and g, walk older and newer changes
:&& repeats the last :s and keeps flags
Use it on a new range or Visual selection

tips.summary: Control fold depth with zA / zr / zm
details: zr opens one level; zm closes one level
Great for notes, snippets, and diagnostics
Use if has('ide') ... endif in shared vimrc files
Keep <Action>(...) maps and IdeaVim options inside
Lets Vim or Neovim keep separate fallbacks
Plugin setup docs recommend this after new set commands

tips.summary: Reuse your Vim config with source ~/.vimrc
details: Keep shared motions and mappings in one place
Wrap IDE-only parts with if has('ide')

tips.summary: Keep your mode during IDE rename edits
details: Rename and template fields stop forcing Select mode
IdeaVim stays in your current mode instead
vif selects inner function; yaf yanks a function
Useful for large methods and quick refactors
Press <C-n> repeatedly to add the next match
Use gs on true/false, &&/||, or similar pairs
Great for fast boolean and operator flips
In Insert mode, Ctrl-n picks the next word match
Good for repeating names already in the file

tips.summary: Trigger IntelliJ completion from Insert Ctrl-Space
details: Ctrl-Space opens basic code completion in IntelliJ
Press it again to widen the suggestion list
IdeaVim supports the IDE completion lookup popup

tips.summary: Completion popup keys are controlled by lookupkeys
details: :set lookupkeys? shows which keys the IDE handles in completion
By default keys like Tab and Enter go to the IDE lookup popup
Keep keys in lookupkeys unless you want IdeaVim to intercept them
